en.extract:
The black-and-crimson oriole (Oriolus consanguineus) is a species of bird in the family Oriolidae.
It is found in Indonesia and Malaysia where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


Taxonomy and systematics
The black-and-crimson oriole was originally described in the genus Leptopteryx (a synonym for Artamella). Along with the black, maroon and silver orioles, it belongs to a clade of red and black orioles. Alternate names for the black-and-crimson oriole include the black-crimson oriole and crimson-breasted oriole.


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized: 

O. c. malayanus - Robinson & Kloss, 1923: Found on the Malay Peninsula
O. c. consanguineus - (Ramsay, RGW, 1881): Originally described as a separate species. Found on Sumatra
O. c. vulneratus - Sharpe, 1887: Originally described as a separate species. Found on northern Borneo


References


External links
Image at ADW Archived 2 December 2008 at the Wayback Machine

The '''black-and-crimson oriole''' ('''''Oriolus consanguineus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Oriolidae]].

It is found in Indonesia and Malaysia where its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The black-and-crimson oriole was originally described in the genus ''Leptopteryx'' (a synonym for ''[[Artamella]]''). Along with the [[Black oriole|black]], [[Maroon oriole|maroon]] and [[silver oriole]]s, it belongs to a clade of red and black orioles.<ref>{{cite journal|title=Phylogeny and biogeography of Oriolidae (Aves: Passeriformes)|author=Jønsson, KA|author2=Rauri C. K. Bowie|author3=Robert G. Moyle|author4=Martin Irestedt|author5=Les Christidis|author6=Janette A. Norman|author7=Jon Fjeldsa|name-list-style=amp|journal=Ecography|volume=33|pages=232–241|year=2010|issue=2|doi=10.1111/j.1600-0587.2010.06167.x|url=http://www.nrm.se/download/18.25ba04a21296cc434f980005871/J%25C3%25B6nsson%2Bet%2Bal%2BOriolidae.pdf|doi-access=free|bibcode=2010Ecogr..33..232J|archive-date=13 November 2013|access-date=1 March 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131113045024/http://www.nrm.se/download/18.25ba04a21296cc434f980005871/J%25C3%25B6nsson%2Bet%2Bal%2BOriolidae.pdf|url-status=dead}}</ref> Alternate names for the black-and-crimson oriole include the '''black-crimson oriole''' and '''crimson-breasted oriole'''.

===Subspecies===
Three [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 7.1|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.7.1|doi-access=free}}</ref> 
* ''O. c. malayanus'' - <small>[[Herbert C. Robinson|Robinson]] & [[C. Boden Kloss|Kloss]], 1923</small>: Found on the [[Malay Peninsula]]
* ''O. c. consanguineus'' - <small>([[Robert George Wardlaw-Ramsay|Ramsay, RGW]], 1881)</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found on [[Sumatra]]
* ''O. c. vulneratus'' - <small>[[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1887</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found on northern [[Borneo]]
